name: Thai Mussel Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot with lid, combine white wine, lemon juice, onion, celery, bay leaf, and cilantro sprigs.
2. Bring to a boil, add mussels, cover, and steam until they open (3-4 minutes). Do not overcook; the mussels will undergo a second cooking process later.
3. Remove mussels with a slotted spoon and cool them.
4. Strain the liquid through a sieve lined with several layers of cheesecloth. The strained liquid should be approximately 3 cups.
5. Break up the noodles and cover with hot water, soaking for 10-15 minutes.
6. In a heavy pot, heat oil. Add crushed chilies, zest, remaining garlic, and ginger. Sauté until tender (1-2 minutes); do not let ingredients brown.
7. Add chopped bok choy and white parts of scallion, cooking another 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. Add clam juice, water, lime juice, fish sauce, soy sauce, sugar, and the strained mussel broth. Bring to a simmer.
9. While heating, remove mussels from shells (reserve any juice, strain it, and add the liquid to the soup). Add the mussels back into the pot.
10. Simmer another 2-3 minutes, garnishing with cilantro and scallion greens.
